purpose: Build an advanced Eclipse plugin surface for Ananta that supports developer workflows, analysis modes, selected Ananta internals, configuration, runtime/control views, approval/audit/repair visibility and optional TUI launch/integration.
depends_on: Eclipse plugin MVP or explicit Eclipse runtime decision
goal: Eclipse should become a serious Ananta workbench surface: useful for coding and analysis, able to inspect selected Ananta internal state, and able to launch or integrate the Ananta TUI without becoming a second unsafe control plane.
success_criteria: Plugin supports multiple analysis/work modes from Eclipse.
Plugin exposes selected Ananta internals such as health, workers, providers, capabilities, queues, tasks, artifacts and traces.
Plugin provides safe configuration views comparable to a subset of the web frontend.
Plugin provides control/admin/runtime views without bypassing Ananta governance.
Plugin exposes approval, audit and repair information deeply enough for real review work.
Plugin can launch Ananta TUI externally or via Eclipse terminal integration.
Complex/deep admin features remain browser-first or read-only unless explicitly safe.
All sensitive actions remain backend-enforced, audited and permission-aware.
architecture_guardrails: Eclipse plugin remains a client surface, not a second Ananta control plane.
No policy, approval, repair execution or worker orchestration logic is duplicated in Eclipse.
Configuration writes require backend permission checks and audit.
Repair execution is never implicit; dry-run/review-first behavior is mandatory.
TUI integration must not create hidden shell execution risk.
Browser fallback remains available for deep or risky admin views.
All views must handle degraded backend state explicitly.

milestones.title: Approval, audit and repair deep views

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse scope boundary define
acceptance_criteria: Document defines which advanced features belong in Eclipse and which remain browser-first.
Document separates developer workflow views from operator/admin/control views.
Document states that Eclipse remains a thin client and not a second control plane.
Document explicitly lists dangerous features that must stay read-only or backend-gated.

tasks.title: Eclipse advanced view model inventory create
acceptance_criteria: Inventory lists all planned Eclipse views and their backend APIs.
Views are grouped into developer, analysis, internals, config, approval, audit, repair and TUI integration.
Each view has read-only/read-write classification.
Missing backend endpoints are listed explicitly instead of mocked silently.

tasks.title: Permission and capability model for Eclipse advanced views define
acceptance_criteria: Plugin can discover backend capabilities and user permissions.
Views disable or hide actions that backend does not allow.
Policy-denied actions are shown clearly.
No Eclipse-side permission assumption can enable backend-denied actions.

tasks.title: Advanced navigation and perspective layout define
acceptance_criteria: A recommended Ananta Eclipse perspective/layout is defined.
Core views include Goal/Analysis, Tasks, Artifacts, Internals, Config, Approvals, Audit, Repair and TUI.
Navigation preserves selected goal/task context.
Open-in-browser fallback is available from deep views.

tasks.title: Analysis mode registry implement
acceptance_criteria: Plugin exposes multiple Ananta analysis modes such as analyze file, analyze project, review selection, patch plan, explain architecture and evolve project.
Modes map to official Ananta goal/task flows.
Modes are capability-aware and disabled when backend does not support them.
Mode registry is testable without Eclipse UI.

tasks.title: Analysis launcher view implement
acceptance_criteria: Eclipse view allows selecting analysis mode and submitting current context.
User sees included file/selection/project context before submit.
Submission creates Ananta goal/task rather than local-only processing.
Result links back to task/artifact views.

tasks.title: Multi-result analysis history view implement
acceptance_criteria: View lists recent analysis runs by mode, status and related project/file.
Users can reopen previous results.
History links to tasks, artifacts and traces.
Stale or deleted backend objects degrade gracefully.

tasks.title: Review and proposal workbench implement
acceptance_criteria: Plugin renders review findings, proposals and change suggestions.
Diff-like artifacts are readable inside Eclipse.
No patch is applied automatically.
Apply-like actions, if later added, require explicit backend approval and user confirmation.

tasks.title: Blueprint and profile selection view implement
acceptance_criteria: View lists available blueprints/work profiles/reference profiles if backend exposes them.
Users can select profile for new/evolve/analyze flows.
Selection is included as explicit metadata in goal submission.
Unavailable profiles are handled without breaking core analysis.

tasks.title: Ananta system overview view implement
acceptance_criteria: View shows backend health, version, active profile, capabilities and degraded state.
View distinguishes local/team/KRITIS-like targets if backend provides that info.
View is read-only by default.
Failure states are explicit and actionable.

tasks.title: Worker and queue visibility view implement
acceptance_criteria: View shows worker availability, queue state and recent worker failures where backend exposes them.
View supports refresh without spamming backend.
No worker control action is available unless backend exposes safe API and permission.
Missing backend endpoint is documented rather than faked.

tasks.title: Provider and model runtime view implement
acceptance_criteria: View shows configured providers/models and important capability state.
Sensitive provider configuration is redacted.
Read-only view works without admin permission.
Write/config actions are not added unless backend supports audited updates.

tasks.title: Task artifact trace explorer implement
acceptance_criteria: View allows navigating goal -> task -> artifact -> trace relationships.
Trace IDs and audit references are visible when present.
Explorer stays readable and does not become full browser clone.
Deep links open corresponding Ananta web UI pages where available.

tasks.title: Runtime diagnostics and degraded-state panel implement
acceptance_criteria: Panel aggregates backend unreachable, auth failed, capability missing, worker unavailable and policy denied states.
Panel provides recommended next action.
Panel never hides degraded state behind generic success.
Tests cover all degraded categories.

tasks.title: Configuration surface strategy define
acceptance_criteria: Document defines which configuration can be shown read-only and which can be edited.
Dangerous settings require backend permission, explicit confirmation and audit.
Some config remains browser-first if Eclipse UX would be unsafe or too complex.
No raw config editor is exposed without validation.

tasks.title: Read-only configuration view implement
acceptance_criteria: View displays safe config categories such as endpoint/profile/capabilities/model summary.
Secrets and tokens are redacted.
Unavailable config categories are shown as unavailable, not empty success.
View is permission-aware.

tasks.title: Safe configuration edit flow implement
acceptance_criteria: Only explicitly safe backend-supported settings can be edited.
Every config update uses backend validation and audit.
User sees diff/preview before apply.
Denied/failed config writes do not modify local assumed state.

tasks.title: Control actions registry implement
acceptance_criteria: Control actions are listed with capability, permission and risk class.
Dangerous actions are disabled unless backend explicitly allows them.
Every action has confirmation and audit reference where needed.
Unknown actions fail closed.

tasks.title: Approval queue advanced view implement
acceptance_criteria: View lists pending approvals with enough context for safe review.
Approve/reject actions call backend approval APIs only.
View shows stale, denied and already-handled states.
Blind approval UX is avoided.

tasks.title: Audit explorer view implement
acceptance_criteria: View shows audit summaries and trace-linked details where backend exposes them.
Sensitive values are redacted.
Filters support action type, severity, time and related task/goal.
Deep audit analysis can open browser fallback.

tasks.title: Repair session explorer implement
acceptance_criteria: View shows repair sessions, diagnosis, proposed steps, dry-run status, approvals, execution results and verification.
Repair execution is never triggered by opening or refreshing the view.
Risk class and approval state are visible.
Unknown/unsafe repair actions are clearly blocked.

tasks.title: Repair review and approval flow implement
acceptance_criteria: Where backend allows it, user can approve/reject repair step from Eclipse.
Approval requires explicit confirmation and shows command/action summary.
Dry-run-first behavior is respected.
Every action returns trace/audit reference.

tasks.title: Policy denial and blocked action view implement
acceptance_criteria: View shows blocked actions and policy denial reasons.
Users can navigate from denial to related task/action/trace.
No workaround path is offered from Eclipse.
View helps debugging without weakening governance.

tasks.title: TUI integration strategy decide
acceptance_criteria: Decision compares external terminal launch, Eclipse Terminal View integration and embedded pseudo-terminal.
Security implications of shell/process launch are documented.
Initial implementation path is selected.
Fallback is open Ananta web UI if TUI cannot be launched safely.

tasks.title: Launch external Ananta TUI action implement
acceptance_criteria: Plugin can launch Ananta TUI using documented TUI command when configured.
Launch command is explicit and user-confirmed.
Endpoint/profile context can be passed safely.
No secrets are exposed in command line where avoidable.

tasks.title: Eclipse terminal integration evaluate and implement if safe
acceptance_criteria: Evaluation checks whether Eclipse Terminal View can host Ananta TUI reliably.
If implemented, TUI opens in an Eclipse terminal with selected profile.
If not implemented, reason is documented and external launch remains supported.
Terminal lifecycle errors are visible.

tasks.title: TUI bridge status view implement
acceptance_criteria: View shows whether TUI is available, command path, selected profile and launch status.
View explains when TUI is preferred over Eclipse views.
Missing TUI runtime degrades clearly.
No fake embedded TUI is presented.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse unit tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover analysis mode registry, permission model, view models and action registry.
Tests cover degraded states and missing capability behavior.
Tests verify read-only/write-gated distinction.
Tests do not require a live Ananta backend.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse integration tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover mocked backend responses for tasks, artifacts, approvals, audit and repair.
Tests verify backend API mapping remains stable.
Tests verify no client-side policy bypass exists.
Tests cover malformed response handling.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse manual smoke checklist add
acceptance_criteria: Checklist covers analysis mode, task/artifact explorer, internals view, config view, approval view, audit view, repair view and TUI launch.
Checklist includes expected results and failure symptoms.
Checklist is short enough for release review.
Checklist distinguishes required and optional advanced features.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse documentation update
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain Eclipse plugin modes and views.
Docs explain what remains browser-first.
Docs explain TUI launch/integration behavior.
Docs warn about permissions, approval and repair safety.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse merge readiness report generate
acceptance_criteria: Report lists implemented advanced views and deferred views.
Report lists required backend endpoints and missing endpoints.
Report includes tests and smoke results.
Report states whether plugin is safe to merge or should stay feature-branch-only.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Define advanced Eclipse boundaries and permission model.
Stage 3: Add selected Ananta internals and runtime visibility.
Stage 4: Add safe configuration and control views.
Stage 5: Add approval, audit and repair deep views.
Stage 6: Add TUI launch or terminal integration.
Stage 7: Add tests, docs and merge readiness report.
summary_notes: This is an advanced Eclipse control-surface track, not the basic Eclipse MVP.
It should run after or alongside the basic Eclipse plugin runtime, but not replace the runtime MVP.
TUI integration should start with explicit launch/external terminal before attempting embedded terminal behavior.
Dangerous admin, repair and configuration actions must remain backend-gated and auditable.
